Data Science¶

1. Import and aggregate data¶

1a. Collect information and Gather all fields you believe might cause avoidable delays¶

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
In [2]:
# Load datasets
ip_airlines = pd.read_excel('Z:\\DS\\Data_science_projects\\Sai\\Capstone2\\python\\Airlines.xlsx')
ip_airports = pd.read_excel('Z:\\DS\\Data_science_projects\\Sai\\Capstone2\\python\\airports.xlsx')
ip_runways = pd.read_excel('Z:\\DS\\Data_science_projects\\Sai\\Capstone2\\python\\runways.xlsx')

In [6]:
# renaming columns for better understanding and consistency
ip_airlines.rename(columns={'Length':'Duration_min'}, inplace=True)
ip_airports.rename(columns={'id':'airport_ref'}, inplace=True)
ip_runways.rename(columns={'id':'runway_id'}, inplace=True)
In [7]:
#checking shape for all datasets
print(ip_airlines.shape)
print(ip_airports.shape)
print(ip_runways.shape)

(518556, 9)
(73805, 18)
(43977, 20)
In [8]:
# checking airports and runways data for important features which cause delays
print(ip_airlines.columns)
print(ip_airports.columns)
print(ip_runways.columns)

Index(['id', 'Airline', 'Flight', 'AirportFrom', 'AirportTo', 'DayOfWeek',
       'Time', 'Duration_min', 'Delay'],
      dtype='object')
Index(['airport_ref', 'ident', 'type', 'name', 'latitude_deg', 'longitude_deg',
       'elevation_ft', 'continent', 'iso_country', 'iso_region',
       'municipality', 'scheduled_service', 'gps_code', 'iata_code',
       'local_code', 'home_link', 'wikipedia_link', 'keywords'],
      dtype='object')
Index(['runway_id', 'airport_ref', 'airport_ident', 'length_ft', 'width_ft',
       'surface', 'lighted', 'closed', 'le_ident', 'le_latitude_deg',
       'le_longitude_deg', 'le_elevation_ft', 'le_heading_degT',
       'le_displaced_threshold_ft', 'he_ident', 'he_latitude_deg',
       'he_longitude_deg', 'he_elevation_ft', 'he_heading_degT',
       'he_displaced_threshold_ft'],
      dtype='object')

Below are the important features from each dataset, might cause avoidable delays in one dataset.

1) ip_airlines: except id all features are important 2) ip_airports: (id, iata_code): to link data to other datasets,(type): size of airport,
(elevation_ft): higher altitudes affect weather & plane performance 3) ip_runways: (airport_ref): to link to the airport data,('length_ft', 'width_ft', 'surface'): determine area and surface type to tell quickness of landing/takeoff, ('lighted', 'closed'): reduces air traffic because of airport limitations which causes delays

In [9]:
#Feature selection
ip_airlines.drop(columns=['id'],inplace=True)
ip_airports = ip_airports[['airport_ref', 'iata_code', 'type', 'elevation_ft']]
ip_runways = ip_runways[['runway_id', 'airport_ref', 'length_ft', 'width_ft', 'surface', 'lighted', 'closed']]

Dropping duplicates

In [10]:
ip_airlines.drop_duplicates(inplace=True)
ip_airports.drop_duplicates(inplace=True)
ip_runways.drop_duplicates(inplace=True)

print(ip_airlines.shape)
print(ip_airports.shape)
print(ip_runways.shape)

#reseting index after dropping duplicates
ip_airlines.reset_index(drop=True, inplace=True)
ip_airports.reset_index(drop=True, inplace=True)
ip_runways.reset_index(drop=True, inplace=True)

(309346, 8)
(73805, 4)
(43977, 7)

Dropping Null values

In [13]:
# Dropping null values as they are around than 5% of the data and we have enough data to train our model without them.
# we cant fill null values because length,width doesnt depend on any other features like lighted/closed/surface/num_runways.
ip_runways.dropna(inplace=True)
ip_runways.reset_index(drop=True, inplace=True)
ip_runways
Out[13]:
runway_id airport_ref length_ft width_ft surface lighted closed

2 254165 6525 2300.0 200.0 TURF 0 0
3 270932 6526 40.0 40.0 GRASS 0 0
4 322128 322127 1450.0 60.0 Turf 0 0
... ... ... ... ... ... ... ...

40929 235169 27244 8530.0 148.0 CON 1 0
40930 346789 346788 1800.0 15.0 Turf 0 0
40931 313663 313629 1713.0 82.0 concrete 0 0

In [15]:
#checking for which airport types iata_code is missing
ip_airports.groupby('type')['iata_code'].count()
# iata_code is missing for mostly smaller size airport types like small_airport, heliport, seaplane_base which are around 3% of the data.
# skipping it for now
Out[15]:
type
balloonport          0
closed             293
heliport            81
large_airport      448
medium_airport    4067
seaplane_base      149
small_airport     4122
Name: iata_code, dtype: int64
In [16]:
import seaborn as sns
# checking distribution of elevation_ft
sns.histplot(ip_airports['elevation_ft'], bins=30, kde=True)
Out[16]:
<Axes: xlabel='elevation_ft', ylabel='Count'>
In [17]:
# filling null values for elevation_ft as data is right skewed and we can use median to fill null values as its robust to outliers
ip_airports['elevation_ft'].fillna(ip_airports['elevation_ft'].median(), inplace=True)

Aggregating Runways data

In [18]:
ip_runways.head(2)
Out[18]:
runway_id airport_ref length_ft width_ft surface lighted closed
0 269408 6523 80.0 80.0 ASPH-G 1 0
1 255155 6524 2500.0 70.0 GRVL 0 0
In [19]:
ip_runways_agg = ip_runways.groupby('airport_ref').agg({
    'runway_id': 'count',
    'length_ft': 'mean',
    'width_ft': 'mean',
    'lighted': 'max',
    'closed': 'max',
    'surface': 'first'
}).reset_index()
ip_runways_agg.rename(columns={'runway_id': 'num_runways'}, inplace=True)
ip_runways_agg.head(5)
Out[19]:
airport_ref num_runways length_ft width_ft lighted closed surface
0 2 1 7080.0 200.0 0 0 ASP
1 3 1 7218.0 148.0 1 0 ASP
2 4 1 4593.0 98.0 0 0 ASP
3 5 1 3855.0 228.0 0 0 GRS
4 7 2 3266.5 250.0 0 0 CON
In [20]:
ip_runways_agg.shape
Out[20]:
(34773, 7)

Joining Airports and Runways aggregated data to get airport wise stats

In [21]:
ip_airports_runways_agg = ip_airports.merge(ip_runways_agg, left_on='airport_ref', right_on='airport_ref', how='inner')
ip_airports_runways_agg.head(5)
Out[21]:
airport_ref iata_code type elevation_ft num_runways length_ft width_ft lighted closed surface
0 6523 NaN heliport 11.0 1 80.0 80.0 1 0 ASPH-G
1 6524 NaN small_airport 450.0 1 2500.0 70.0 0 0 GRVL
2 6525 NaN small_airport 820.0 1 2300.0 200.0 0 0 TURF
3 6526 NaN closed 237.0 1 40.0 40.0 0 0 GRASS
4 322127 NaN small_airport 1100.0 1 1450.0 60.0 0 0 Turf

1b. Scraping airlines data¶

In [24]:
import requests
url_airlines = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_airlines_of_the_United_States"
headers = {
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36"
}
response = requests.get(url_airlines, headers=headers)
tables = pd.read_html(response.text)
In [25]:
len(tables) #total 21 tables present in the wiki-link
Out[25]:
21
In [26]:
ref_airline_exp = pd.concat([tables[0], tables[1], tables[2], tables[3]], ignore_index=True)
ref_airline_exp['Exp_Years'] = 2026 - ref_airline_exp['Founded']
ref_airline_exp=ref_airline_exp[['Airline', 'IATA', 'Founded', 'Exp_Years']]
ref_airline_exp.rename(columns={'Airline': 'Airline_name', 'IATA': 'iata_code'}, inplace=True)
ref_airline_exp
Out[26]:
Airline_name iata_code Founded Exp_Years
0 Alaska Air Group AS 1932.0 94.0
1 Allegiant Air G4 1997.0 29.0
2 American Airlines AA 1926.0 100.0
3 Avelo Airlines XP 1987.0 39.0
4 Breeze Airways MX 2018.0 8.0
... ... ... ... ...
81 Superior Aviation SO 1979.0 47.0
82 Talkeetna Air Taxi NaN 1947.0 79.0
83 Tropic Ocean Airways TI 2009.0 17.0
84 World Atlantic Airlines WL 2002.0 24.0
85 XOJET Aviation LLC NaN 2006.0 20.0

86 rows × 4 columns

Merging airlines_experience_df data with ip_airlines

In [28]:
print(ip_airlines.head(2))
print(ref_airline_exp.head(2))

  Airline  Flight AirportFrom AirportTo  DayOfWeek  Time  Duration_min  Delay
0      CO     269         SFO       IAH          3    15           205      1
1      US    1558         PHX       CLT          3    15           222      1
       Airline_name iata_code  Founded  Exp_Years
0  Alaska Air Group        AS   1932.0       94.0
1     Allegiant Air        G4   1997.0       29.0
In [29]:
ip_airlines_exp=ip_airlines.merge(ref_airline_exp[['Airline_name','iata_code','Exp_Years']], left_on='Airline', right_on='iata_code', how='left')
ip_airlines_exp.drop(columns=['iata_code'],inplace=True)
ip_airlines_exp.head(5)
Out[29]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years
0 CO 269 SFO IAH 3 15 205 1 NaN NaN
1 US 1558 PHX CLT 3 15 222 1 NaN NaN
2 AA 2400 LAX DFW 3 20 165 1 American Airlines 100.0
3 AA 2466 SFO DFW 3 20 195 1 American Airlines 100.0
4 AS 108 ANC SEA 3 30 202 0 Alaska Air Group 94.0
In [30]:

print(ip_airlines_exp.isnull().sum())
ip_airlines_exp.isnull().sum() * 100 / ip_airlines_exp.shape[0]

In [31]:
# we can see that around 53 thousand airlines has missing experience years,its 17 % out of all data. hence cant be dropped. 
# checking distribution of experience years of airlines
sns.histplot(ip_airlines_exp['Exp_Years'], bins=30, kde=True)
Out[31]:
<Axes: xlabel='Exp_Years', ylabel='Count'>
In [32]:
# Distribution of Experience Years is not normally distributed so,
# we will replace missing values with median value because it wont be biased towards old or new airlines.
ip_airlines_exp['Exp_Years'].fillna(ip_airlines_exp['Exp_Years'].median(),inplace=True)
In [33]:
ip_airlines_exp
Out[33]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years

2 AA 2400 LAX DFW 3 20 165 1 American Airlines 100.0
3 AA 2466 SFO DFW 3 20 195 1 American Airlines 100.0
4 AS 108 ANC SEA 3 30 202 0 Alaska Air Group 94.0
... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ...

1d. Web scraping Passenger traffic data from the Wikipedia page¶

NOTE: web scraping traffic data for airlines first before joining/ merging all data(airlines,airports,runways)

In [34]:
import requests
url_traffic = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_the_busiest_airports_in_the_United_States"
headers = {
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36"
}
response = requests.get(url_traffic, headers=headers)
tables = pd.read_html(response.text)
len(tables) #total 10 tables present in the wiki-link
Out[34]:
11
In [35]:
#renaming columns for consistency
tables[0].rename(columns={'Airports (large)': 'Airport_name','Major cities served': 'City served','Metro area':'Metro Area','IATA Code':'iata_code','2024[2]':'2024'},inplace=True)
tables[1].rename(columns={'Airports (medium hubs)': 'Airport_name','IATA Code':'iata_code','2024[2]':'2024'},inplace=True)
In [36]:
#adding labels for large and medium hubs
tables[0]['hub_type'] = 'Large'
tables[1]['hub_type'] = 'Medium'
In [37]:
ref_airline_hub=pd.concat([tables[0], tables[1]], ignore_index=True)
print(ref_airline_hub.shape)
ref_airline_hub.head(2)

(82, 22)
Out[37]:
Rank Airport City served State IATA/ICAO Passengers hub_type Rank (2024) Airports (large) iata_code ... 2024 2023[3] 2022[4] 2021[5] 2020[6] 2019[7] 2018[8] 2017[9] 2016[10] 2015[11]
0 1.0 Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport Atlanta Georgia ATL/KATL 106302208.0 Large NaN NaN NaN ... NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
1 2.0 Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport Dallas, Fort Worth Texas DFW/KDFW 85660127.0 Large NaN NaN NaN ... NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN

2 rows × 22 columns

In [38]:
# taking latest year 2024 for calculating percentage change in passenger traffic to check percentage change in passenger traffic 
ref_airline_hub=ref_airline_hub[['iata_code', '2024', 'hub_type']]
total_traffic = ref_airline_hub['2024'].sum()
print('total traffic in 2024:', total_traffic)
ref_airline_hub['%traffic'] = (ref_airline_hub['2024'] / total_traffic) * 100

total traffic in 2024: 702968069.0
In [39]:
print(ref_airline_hub['%traffic'][ref_airline_hub['hub_type']=='Large'].describe())
print(ref_airline_hub['%traffic'][ref_airline_hub['hub_type']=='Medium'].describe())

count    0.0
mean     NaN
std      NaN
min      NaN
25%      NaN
50%      NaN
75%      NaN
max      NaN
Name: %traffic, dtype: float64
count    31.000000
mean      3.225806
std       1.556712
min       1.473764
25%       1.865284
50%       3.000239
75%       3.990562
max       7.469956
Name: %traffic, dtype: float64

Labeling hub_type large and medium hubs based on passenger traffic % is correct as per two conditions

1 Large hubs are airports that account for at least 1 % of the total passenger enplanements in the United States.

2 Airports that account for 0.25 % to 1 % of total passenger enplanements are considered medium hubs.

In [40]:
ip_airlines_exp.head(2)
Out[40]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years
0 CO 269 SFO IAH 3 15 205 1 NaN 59.0
1 US 1558 PHX CLT 3 15 222 1 NaN 59.0
In [41]:
ref_airline_hub.head(2)
Out[41]:
iata_code 2024 hub_type %traffic
0 NaN NaN Large NaN
1 NaN NaN Large NaN
In [42]:
# Match traffic info to the departure airport
ip_airlines_exp_hub=ip_airlines_exp.merge(ref_airline_hub[['iata_code', 'hub_type']], left_on='AirportFrom', right_on='iata_code', how='left')
# Match traffic info to the arrival airport
ip_airlines_exp_hub=ip_airlines_exp_hub.merge(ref_airline_hub[['iata_code', 'hub_type']], left_on='AirportTo', right_on='iata_code', how='left')
ip_airlines_exp_hub.head(5)
Out[42]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years iata_code_x hub_type_x iata_code_y hub_type_y
0 CO 269 SFO IAH 3 15 205 1 NaN 59.0 SFO Medium IAH Medium
1 US 1558 PHX CLT 3 15 222 1 NaN 59.0 PHX Medium CLT Medium
2 AA 2400 LAX DFW 3 20 165 1 American Airlines 100.0 LAX Medium DFW Medium
3 AA 2466 SFO DFW 3 20 195 1 American Airlines 100.0 SFO Medium DFW Medium
4 AS 108 ANC SEA 3 30 202 0 Alaska Air Group 94.0 NaN NaN SEA Medium
In [43]:
ip_airlines_exp_hub.rename(columns={'hub_type_x': 'src_hub_type','hub_type_y': 'dest_hub_type'}, inplace=True)
ip_airlines_exp_hub.drop(columns=['iata_code_x', 'iata_code_y'], inplace=True)
ip_airlines_exp_hub.head(5)
Out[43]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years src_hub_type dest_hub_type
0 CO 269 SFO IAH 3 15 205 1 NaN 59.0 Medium Medium
1 US 1558 PHX CLT 3 15 222 1 NaN 59.0 Medium Medium
2 AA 2400 LAX DFW 3 20 165 1 American Airlines 100.0 Medium Medium
3 AA 2466 SFO DFW 3 20 195 1 American Airlines 100.0 Medium Medium
4 AS 108 ANC SEA 3 30 202 0 Alaska Air Group 94.0 NaN Medium

In [45]:
# we can see that remaining hub types are small/other so we will replace null values with 'small_other'
ip_airlines_exp_hub['src_hub_type'].fillna('small_other', inplace=True)
ip_airlines_exp_hub['dest_hub_type'].fillna('small_other', inplace=True)
In [46]:
ip_airlines_exp_hub.isnull().sum()

1c. FINAL Merging airlines,airport,runways to get all the information¶

In [47]:
ip_airports_runways_agg.head(2)
Out[47]:
airport_ref iata_code type elevation_ft num_runways length_ft width_ft lighted closed surface
0 6523 NaN heliport 11.0 1 80.0 80.0 1 0 ASPH-G
1 6524 NaN small_airport 450.0 1 2500.0 70.0 0 0 GRVL
In [48]:
ip_airlines_exp_hub.head(2)
Out[48]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years src_hub_type dest_hub_type
0 CO 269 SFO IAH 3 15 205 1 NaN 59.0 Medium Medium
1 US 1558 PHX CLT 3 15 222 1 NaN 59.0 Medium Medium
In [49]:
df = ip_airlines_exp_hub.merge(ip_airports_runways_agg, left_on='AirportFrom', right_on='iata_code', how='left')
df.rename(columns={col: f'src_{col}' for col in ip_airports_runways_agg.columns}, inplace=True)

df = df.merge(ip_airports_runways_agg, left_on='AirportTo', right_on='iata_code', how='left')
df.rename(columns={col: f'dest_{col}' for col in ip_airports_runways_agg.columns if not col.startswith('src_')}, inplace=True)
df.head(2)
Out[49]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years ... dest_airport_ref dest_iata_code dest_type dest_elevation_ft dest_num_runways dest_length_ft dest_width_ft dest_lighted dest_closed dest_surface
0 CO 269 SFO IAH 3 15 205 1 NaN 59.0 ... 3604.0 IAH large_airport 97.0 5.0 10080.4 150.0 1.0 0.0 CON
1 US 1558 PHX CLT 3 15 222 1 NaN 59.0 ... 3457.0 CLT large_airport 748.0 4.0 9044.5 150.0 1.0 0.0 PEM

2 rows × 32 columns

In [52]:
# around 0.12 percent null values present hence we can drop
df.dropna(inplace=True)
df.reset_index(drop=True, inplace=True)
# also dropping unwanted features
df.drop(columns=['src_iata_code','dest_iata_code','src_airport_ref','dest_airport_ref'],inplace=True)

2. Missing Values & Outliers handling¶

NOTE: missing values are already handled while merging different datasets in Task 1. So only outliers are handled below.

In [55]:
subset_df=df[['Time','Duration_min', 'Exp_Years', 'src_elevation_ft', 'src_length_ft', 'src_width_ft', 'dest_elevation_ft', 'dest_length_ft', 'dest_width_ft']]
In [56]:
#outliers detection using boxplot
plt.figure(figsize=(15,10))
for i in range(len(subset_df.columns)):
    plt.subplot(3,3,i+1)
    sns.boxplot(subset_df[subset_df.columns[i]],orient='h')

Looking at all boxplots , features 'Time' and 'Exp_Years' doesnt have outliers, but other features have some outliers which we will remove.

In [57]:
outliers_elevation = df[(df['src_elevation_ft'] >= 8000) | (df['dest_elevation_ft'] >= 8000)].shape[0]
outliers_length = df[(df['src_length_ft'] <= 4100) | (df['dest_length_ft'] <= 4100)].shape[0]
outliers_width = df[(df['src_width_ft'] >= 300) | (df['dest_width_ft'] >= 300)].shape[0]
outliers_duration = df[df['Duration_min'] >= 600].shape[0]
print('Total Outliers')
print(f"Rows with elevation >= 8000ft: {outliers_elevation}")
print(f"Rows with runway length <= 4100ft: {outliers_length}")
print(f"Rows with runway width >= 300ft: {outliers_width}")
print(f"Rows with duration >= 600min: {outliers_duration}")

Total Outliers
Rows with elevation >= 8000ft: 34
Rows with runway length <= 4100ft: 97
Rows with runway width >= 300ft: 786
Rows with duration >= 600min: 17
In [58]:
# removing outliers based on above conditions
df = df[(df['src_elevation_ft'] < 8000) & (df['dest_elevation_ft'] < 8000)]
df = df[(df['src_length_ft'] > 4100) & (df['dest_length_ft'] > 4100)]
df = df[(df['src_width_ft'] < 300) & (df['dest_width_ft'] < 300)]
df = df[df['Duration_min'] < 600]
df.reset_index(drop=True, inplace=True)
In [59]:
df.shape
Out[59]:
(254929, 28)

3. Data visualization and insights¶

3.1 Delay by Airlines¶

In [60]:
df.head(2)
Out[60]:
Airline Flight AirportFrom AirportTo DayOfWeek Time Duration_min Delay Airline_name Exp_Years ... src_closed src_surface dest_type dest_elevation_ft dest_num_runways dest_length_ft dest_width_ft dest_lighted dest_closed dest_surface
0 AA 2400 LAX DFW 3 20 165 1 American Airlines 100.0 ... 0.0 CON large_airport 607.0 7.0 11486.142857 171.428571 1.0 0.0 CON
1 AA 2466 SFO DFW 3 20 195 1 American Airlines 100.0 ... 0.0 ASP large_airport 607.0 7.0 11486.142857 171.428571 1.0 0.0 CON

2 rows × 28 columns

In [61]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.title('Delay by Airline')
plt.ylim(0, 1)
plt.yticks(np.arange(0, 1.1, 0.1))
sns.barplot(data=df,x='Airline',y='Delay',palette='viridis')
plt.show()

According to barplot, approximately 70% of Southwest Airlines flights are delayed

3.2 Daywise Flight delays¶

In [62]:
(df['DayOfWeek'][df['Delay']==1]).value_counts() / df['DayOfWeek'].value_counts()
Out[62]:
1    0.500055
2    0.493252
3    0.510572
4    0.491200
5    0.498920
6    0.433584
7    0.485990
Name: DayOfWeek, dtype: float64
In [63]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.title('Daywise Flight delays')
plt.yticks(np.arange(0, 1.1, 0.05))
sns.barplot(data=df,x='DayOfWeek',y='Delay',palette='magma')
plt.show()

We can see from graph that Day 6 (Saturday) has the lowest delays. Hence Saturday is the safest for travel.

3.3 Airlines recommendation for short, medium and long-distance travel¶

NOTE: Assuming -
Short: < 90 minutes(less than 1.5 hrs) , Medium: 90-180 minutes(1.5-3 hrs) , Long: > 180 minutes(more than 3 hrs)

In [64]:
df.loc[df['Duration_min']<90, 'Distance_type'] = 'Short'
df.loc[(df['Duration_min']>=90) & (df['Duration_min']<180), 'Distance_type'] = 'Medium'
df.loc[df['Duration_min']>=180, 'Distance_type'] = 'Long'
In [65]:
# df['Distance_type']=pd.cut(df['Duration_min'],bins=[-np.inf,89,180,np.inf],labels=['Short','Medium','Long'])
In [66]:
df['Distance_type']
Out[66]:
0         Medium
1           Long
2           Long
3           Long
4           Long
           ...  
254924      Long
254925      Long
254926      Long
254927      Long
254928      Long
Name: Distance_type, Length: 254929, dtype: object
In [67]:
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 6))
plt.title('Airlines performance for short, medium and long-distance travel')
plt.ylim(0, 1)
plt.yticks(np.arange(0, 1.1, 0.05))
sns.barplot(data=df,x='Distance_type',y='Delay',hue='Airline',palette='Set2')
plt.show()
In [68]:
print(df['Airline_name'][df['Airline']=='YV'][:1])
print(df['Airline_name'][df['Airline']=='UA'][:1])
print(df['Airline_name'][df['Airline']=='9E'][:1])

50    Mesa Airlines
Name: Airline_name, dtype: object
51    United Airlines
Name: Airline_name, dtype: object
22    Endeavor Air
Name: Airline_name, dtype: object

As per graph we can say that Airlines recommendation is YV (Mesa Airlines) for short distance , UA (United Airlines) for medium distance travel and 9E (Endeavor Air) for long-distance travel.

4. Flights delayed at large hubs compared to medium hubs¶

Hub_types are 'Large', 'Medium', 'small_other'. Need only 'Large' and 'Medium'. Also, need to check for both source hub_type delay and destination hub_type delay.

In [69]:
hub_filter = df[ (df['src_hub_type'].isin(['Large', 'Medium'])) & (df['dest_hub_type'].isin(['Large', 'Medium'])) ]
In [70]:
hub_delay=hub_filter[hub_filter['Delay']==1].groupby(['src_hub_type','dest_hub_type'])['Delay'].count().unstack()
hub_delay
# df.groupby(['src_hub_type','dest_hub_type'])['Delay'].sum().unstack() we can also use this as Delay is 1 or 0
Out[70]:
dest_hub_type Medium
src_hub_type
Medium 42654
In [71]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.title('Flights delayed at large hubs Vs medium hubs')
sns.heatmap(hub_delay, annot=True, fmt='g', cmap='Blues')
plt.xlabel('Destination Hub Type')
plt.ylabel('Source Hub Type')
plt.show()

From heatmap, we can see that,

Source: Large hubs

1) Maximum number of delayed flights observed is 42654, from Large source hubs to Large destination hubs. 2) Total delayed flights from Large source hubs to Medium destination hubs are 18721

Source:Medium hubs

1) Total delayed Flights from Medium source hubs to Large destination hubs are 17587. 2) Minimum number of delayed flights observed is 5895 Medium source hubs to Medium destination hubs.

5. Hypothesis Testing¶

5.1 If the airport's altitude has anything to do with flight delays for incoming and departing flights¶

1 For source altitude:¶

1) Null hypothesis H0: Source airport altitude has NO significant effect on flight delays. 2) Alternate Hypothesis H1 : Source airport altitude SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

2 For destination altitude:¶

1) Null hypothesis H0 : Destination airport altitude has NO significant effect on flight delays. 2) Alternate Hypothesis H1 : Destination airport altitude SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

In [72]:
# we will use these columns df[['src_elevation_ft','dest_elevation_ft','Delay']]
# also we will be using One sample T test as we need to check both incoming and outgoing flights
In [73]:
from scipy.stats import ttest_ind
elev_delayed_src = df[df['Delay'] == 1]['src_elevation_ft']
elev_ontime_src = df[df['Delay'] == 0]['src_elevation_ft']

elev_delayed_dest = df[df['Delay'] == 1]['dest_elevation_ft']
elev_ontime_dest = df[df['Delay'] == 0]['dest_elevation_ft']

print('Source Altitude effect on Delay:', ttest_ind(elev_delayed_src, elev_ontime_src, equal_var=False))
print('Destination Altitude effect on Delay:', ttest_ind(elev_delayed_dest, elev_ontime_dest, equal_var=False))

Source Altitude effect on Delay: TtestResult(statistic=-0.4600198277945197, pvalue=0.6455023811163216, df=254781.3031763158)
Destination Altitude effect on Delay: TtestResult(statistic=0.8997622913509047, pvalue=0.36824761665605177, df=254629.23408227984)

Conclusion:

1) From T-tests to check Source Altitude effect on Delay: --> P value is greater than 5 percent hence we fail to reject the null hypothesis H0 which is, Source airport altitude has NO significant effect on flight delays.

2) From T-tests to check Destination Altitude effect on Delay: --> P value is greater than 5 percent hence we fail to reject the null hypothesis H0 which is, Destination airport altitude has NO significant effect on flight delays.

5.2 If the number of runways at an airport affects flight delays¶

For no of source runways: 1) Null hypothesis H0: Number of source runways has NO significant effect on flight delays. 2) Alternate Hypothesis H1 : Number of source runways SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

For no of destination runways: 1) Null hypothesis H0 : Number of Destination runways has NO significant effect on flight delays. 2) Alternate Hypothesis H1 : Number of Destination runways SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

In [74]:
# we will use these columns df[['src_num_runways','dest_num_runways','Delay']]
# also we will be using One sample T test as we need to check both no of runways at source and destination.
In [75]:
# 1. Source Airport Runways Test
runways_delayed_src = df[df['Delay'] == 1]['src_num_runways']
runways_ontime_src = df[df['Delay'] == 0]['src_num_runways']

# 2. Destination Airport Runways Test
runways_delayed_dest = df[df['Delay'] == 1]['dest_num_runways']
runways_ontime_dest = df[df['Delay'] == 0]['dest_num_runways']

print('Source Runways effect on Delay:', ttest_ind(runways_delayed_src, runways_ontime_src, equal_var=False))
print('Destination Runways effect on Delay:', ttest_ind(runways_delayed_dest, runways_ontime_dest, equal_var=False))

Source Runways effect on Delay: TtestResult(statistic=2.5499734207250917, pvalue=0.010773693082830181, df=254863.9680972707)
Destination Runways effect on Delay: TtestResult(statistic=-27.040753716180703, pvalue=8.301391372285701e-161, df=254368.87799687876)

Conclusion:

1) From T-tests to check Number of source runways effect on Delay: --> P value is less than 5 percent hence we will reject the null hypothesis H0 and accept the alternate hypothesis H1 which is,Number of source runways SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

2) From T-tests to check Number of destination runways effect on Delay: --> P value is less than 5 percent hence we will reject the null hypothesis H0 and accept the alternate hypothesis H1 which is,Number of destination runways SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

5.3 If the duration of a flight (length) affects flight delays¶

1) Null hypothesis H0: Duration of a flight has NO significant effect on flight delays. 2) Alternate Hypothesis H1 : Duration of a flight SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

In [76]:
# we will use these columns df[['Duration_min', 'Delay']]
# also we will be using One sample T test , original 'Length' column was renamed to 'Duration_min' earlier in this same notebook.
In [77]:
duration_delayed = df[df['Delay'] == 1]['Duration_min']
duration_ontime = df[df['Delay'] == 0]['Duration_min']

print('Duration(length) effect on Delay:', ttest_ind(duration_delayed, duration_ontime, equal_var=False))

Duration(length) effect on Delay: TtestResult(statistic=2.9129656056709257, pvalue=0.0035804515428449427, df=254465.7464114102)

Conclusion:

From T-tests to check Duration(length) effect on Delay: --> P value is less than 5 percent hence we will reject the null hypothesis H0 and accept the alternate hypothesis H1 which is,Duration of a flight SIGNIFICANTLY affects flight delays.

6. correlation matrix between the flight delay predictors¶

In [78]:
corr_df=df[df.select_dtypes(include=np.number).columns]
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))
sns.heatmap(corr_df.corr(),annot=True,fmt='.2f',cmap='coolwarm')
Out[78]:
<Axes: >

Looking at the Delay row/column, we find that very weak linear correlations exists between Delay and other numerical features. It means that these features may not have a strong linear relationship with flight delays and may have nonlinear relationship.

Also not single feature is having very strong linear correlation with other features. Hence we wont drop any features.

In [79]:
# Exporting for sql database
# df.to_csv('Z:\\DS\\Data_science_projects\\Sai\\Capstone2\\sql\\cleaned_flight_data.csv', index=False)

Machine Learning¶

1. OneHotEncoder and OrdinalEncoder to deal with categorical variables¶

    print("\n")

In [83]:
# we will drop 'AirportFrom' and 'AirportTo' as they have too many unique values 
df.drop(columns=[ 'Airline_name', 'AirportFrom', 'AirportTo'], inplace=True)
In [84]:
# we will use One-Hot Encoding to these columns : 'Distance_type', 'src_hub_type' ,dest_hub_type'
cols_ohe = ['Airline', 'src_surface', 'dest_surface']
# we will use Ordinal Encoding to these columns : 'Distance_type','src_hub_type', 'dest_hub_type', 'src_type', 'dest_type'
cols_ordinal = ['Distance_type', 'src_hub_type', 'dest_hub_type', 'src_type', 'dest_type']

OneHotEncoding

In [85]:
# we will use pd.getdummies for one hot encoding
df=pd.get_dummies(df, columns=cols_ohe, drop_first=True)

Ordinal Encoding

In [86]:
dist_order = ['Short', 'Medium', 'Long']
hub_order  = ['small_other', 'Medium', 'Large']
type_order = ['small_airport', 'medium_airport', 'large_airport']

from sklearn.preprocessing import OrdinalEncoder
ord_encoder = OrdinalEncoder(categories=[dist_order, hub_order, hub_order, type_order, type_order])
df[cols_ordinal] = ord_encoder.fit_transform(df[cols_ordinal])
In [87]:
#final checking if all categorical features are encoded or not
df.info()

memory usage: 52.5 MB

2. Performing the following model building steps:¶

In [88]:
# checking data imbalnce for target variable 'Delay'
print(df['Delay'].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100)

0    51.03421
1    48.96579
Name: Delay, dtype: float64

we can say that data is balanced well

2.1 Split data into train and test¶

In [89]:
x=df.drop(columns='Delay')
y=df['Delay']
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
x_train,x_test,y_train,y_test= train_test_split(x,y,test_size=0.2,random_state=42)

2.2 Standardize data¶

In [90]:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
ss=StandardScaler()
x_train_scaled=ss.fit_transform(x_train)
x_test_scaled=ss.transform(x_test)

2.3 Applying logistic regression (use stochastic gradient descent optimizer) and decision tree models¶

In [91]:
from sklearn.linear_model import SGDClassifier
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, cross_val_score
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, confusion_matrix, accuracy_score
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.model_selection import RandomizedSearchCV

2.31 Logistic Regression using stochastic gradient descent) Model¶

In [92]:
skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
# creating pipeline as per instructions to ensure clean code and no data leakage  
pipe_logistic = Pipeline([
    ('scaler', StandardScaler()),
    ('sgd', SGDClassifier(loss='log_loss', random_state=42))
])
logistic_cv = cross_val_score(pipe_logistic, x_train, y_train, cv=skf).mean()
pipe_logistic.fit(x_train, y_train)
logistic_train = pipe_logistic.score(x_train, y_train)
logistic_test = pipe_logistic.score(x_test, y_test)
logistic_y_test_pred = pipe_logistic.predict(x_test)
print('Logistic Regression Cross-Validation accuracy: ', logistic_cv)
print('Logistic Regression Training accuracy: ', logistic_train)
print('Logistic Regression Test accuracy: ', logistic_test)
print('Logistic Regression Classification Report: ', '\n', classification_report(y_test, logistic_y_test_pred))
print('Logistic Regression Confusion Matrix: ', '\n', confusion_matrix(y_test, logistic_y_test_pred))

Logistic Regression Cross-Validation accuracy:  0.5837758251489277
Logistic Regression Training accuracy:  0.5846339418366897
Logistic Regression Test accuracy:  0.5854156042835288
Logistic Regression Classification Report:  
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.59      0.62      0.61     26071
           1       0.58      0.54      0.56     24915

    accuracy                           0.59     50986
   macro avg       0.59      0.58      0.58     50986
weighted avg       0.59      0.59      0.58     50986

Logistic Regression Confusion Matrix:  
 [[16274  9797]
 [11341 13574]]

Hyper parameter tuning of Logistic Regression using stochastic gradient descent) model

In [93]:
param={'sgd__alpha': [0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1], 'sgd__learning_rate': ['constant', 'optimal'], 'sgd__penalty': ['l2', 'l1', 'elasticnet']}
random_sgd= RandomizedSearchCV(pipe_logistic,param,cv=skf)
random_sgd.fit(x_train,y_train)
print("Best parameters for SGD Classifier:", random_sgd.best_params_)

Best parameters for SGD Classifier: {'sgd__penalty': 'l2', 'sgd__learning_rate': 'optimal', 'sgd__alpha': 0.01}
In [94]:
skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
pipe_logistic = Pipeline([
    ('scaler', StandardScaler()),
    ('sgd', SGDClassifier(loss='log_loss', random_state=42, penalty='l2', learning_rate='optimal', alpha=0.01))
])
logistic_cv = cross_val_score(pipe_logistic, x_train, y_train, cv=skf).mean()
pipe_logistic.fit(x_train, y_train)
logistic_train = pipe_logistic.score(x_train, y_train)
logistic_test = pipe_logistic.score(x_test, y_test)
logistic_y_test_pred = pipe_logistic.predict(x_test)
print('After Hyperparameter Tuning:',end='\n')
print('Logistic Regression Cross-Validation accuracy: ', logistic_cv)
print('Logistic Regression Training accuracy: ', logistic_train)
print('Logistic Regression Test accuracy: ', logistic_test)
print('Logistic Regression Classification Report: ', '\n', classification_report(y_test, logistic_y_test_pred))
print('Logistic Regression Confusion Matrix: ', '\n', confusion_matrix(y_test, logistic_y_test_pred))

After Hyperparameter Tuning:
Logistic Regression Cross-Validation accuracy:  0.5895568699209601
Logistic Regression Training accuracy:  0.5895912093084833
Logistic Regression Test accuracy:  0.5925548189699134
Logistic Regression Classification Report:  
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.59      0.69      0.63     26071
           1       0.60      0.49      0.54     24915

    accuracy                           0.59     50986
   macro avg       0.59      0.59      0.59     50986
weighted avg       0.59      0.59      0.59     50986

Logistic Regression Confusion Matrix:  
 [[17930  8141]
 [12633 12282]]

2.32 Decision Tree Model¶

In [95]:
skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

# Decision Tree Classifier
pipe_dt = Pipeline([
    ('scaler', StandardScaler()),
    ('dt', DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=42))
])
dt_cv = cross_val_score(pipe_dt, x_train, y_train, cv=skf).mean()
pipe_dt.fit(x_train, y_train)
dt_train = pipe_dt.score(x_train, y_train)
dt_test = pipe_dt.score(x_test, y_test)
dt_y_test_pred = pipe_dt.predict(x_test)
print('Decision Tree Cross-Validation accuracy: ', dt_cv)
print('Decision Tree Training accuracy: ', dt_train)
print('Decision Tree Test accuracy: ', dt_test)
print('Decision Tree Classification Report: ', '\n', classification_report(y_test, dt_y_test_pred))
print('Decision Tree Confusion Matrix: ', '\n', confusion_matrix(y_test, dt_y_test_pred))

Decision Tree Cross-Validation accuracy:  0.37211377934659745
Decision Tree Training accuracy:  0.7643361135219154
Decision Tree Test accuracy:  0.3302279056996038
Decision Tree Classification Report:  
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.36      0.40      0.38     26071
           1       0.29      0.26      0.27     24915

    accuracy                           0.33     50986
   macro avg       0.33      0.33      0.33     50986
weighted avg       0.33      0.33      0.33     50986

Decision Tree Confusion Matrix:  
 [[10379 15692]
 [18457  6458]]

Handling overfitting of decision tree by hyperparameter tuning

In [97]:
param={'dt__max_depth': np.arange(1, 11), 'dt__min_samples_split': np.arange(2, 11), 'dt__min_samples_leaf': np.arange(1, 11)}
random_dt= RandomizedSearchCV(pipe_dt,param,cv=skf)
random_dt.fit(x_train,y_train)
print("Best parameters for Decision Tree Classifier:", random_dt.best_params_)

Best parameters for Decision Tree Classifier: {'dt__min_samples_split': 5, 'dt__min_samples_leaf': 8, 'dt__max_depth': 8}
In [98]:
skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

# Decision Tree Classifier
pipe_dt = Pipeline([
    ('scaler', StandardScaler()),
    ('dt', DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=42, min_samples_split=6, min_samples_leaf=2, max_depth=10))
])
dt_cv = cross_val_score(pipe_dt, x_train, y_train, cv=skf).mean()
pipe_dt.fit(x_train, y_train)
dt_train = pipe_dt.score(x_train, y_train)
dt_test = pipe_dt.score(x_test, y_test)
dt_y_test_pred = pipe_dt.predict(x_test)
print('After Hyperparameter Tuning:',end='\n')
print('Decision Tree Cross-Validation accuracy: ', dt_cv)
print('Decision Tree Training accuracy: ', dt_train)
print('Decision Tree Test accuracy: ', dt_test)
print('Decision Tree Classification Report: ', '\n', classification_report(y_test, dt_y_test_pred))
print('Decision Tree Confusion Matrix: ', '\n', confusion_matrix(y_test, dt_y_test_pred))

After Hyperparameter Tuning:
Decision Tree Cross-Validation accuracy:  0.5849526406575862
Decision Tree Training accuracy:  0.6033793756098518
Decision Tree Test accuracy:  0.5900247126662221
Decision Tree Classification Report:  
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.58      0.75      0.65     26071
           1       0.62      0.42      0.50     24915

    accuracy                           0.59     50986
   macro avg       0.60      0.59      0.58     50986
weighted avg       0.60      0.59      0.58     50986

Decision Tree Confusion Matrix:  
 [[19588  6483]
 [14420 10495]]

Comparison of logistic regression model with decision tree model

1) Logistic Regression is better model than decision tree because it captures more actual delays (12282 correct delay predictions vs. 10495 for the Decision Tree).

2) Also we can see that,for class 1(flights delayed) , Logistic Regression Model achieved a higher recall of 0.49 compared to the Decision Tree Model 0.42

3) Accuracy for decision tree model is slightly higher (60.3 percent) as compared to Logistic regression model (58.9 percent)

3. Building and validating the models using the Gradient Boosting classifier, comparing all methods, and sharing findings¶

In [99]:
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier
# Gradient Boosting Classifier
pipe_gb = Pipeline([
    ('scaler', StandardScaler()),
    ('gb', GradientBoostingClassifier(random_state=42))
])
gb_cv = cross_val_score(pipe_gb, x_train, y_train, cv=skf).mean()
pipe_gb.fit(x_train, y_train)
gb_train = pipe_gb.score(x_train, y_train)
gb_test = pipe_gb.score(x_test, y_test)
gb_y_test_pred = pipe_gb.predict(x_test)
print('Gradient Boosting Cross-Validation accuracy: ', gb_cv)
print('Gradient Boosting Training accuracy: ', gb_train)
print('Gradient Boosting Test accuracy: ', gb_test)
print('Gradient Boosting Classification Report: ', '\n', classification_report(y_test, gb_y_test_pred))
print('Gradient Boosting Confusion Matrix: ', '\n', confusion_matrix(y_test, gb_y_test_pred))

Gradient Boosting Cross-Validation accuracy:  0.5970785860791542
Gradient Boosting Training accuracy:  0.6004864104185974
Gradient Boosting Test accuracy:  0.601380771192092
Gradient Boosting Classification Report:  
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.59      0.74      0.66     26071
           1       0.63      0.46      0.53     24915

    accuracy                           0.60     50986
   macro avg       0.61      0.60      0.59     50986
weighted avg       0.61      0.60      0.59     50986

Gradient Boosting Confusion Matrix:  
 [[19300  6771]
 [13553 11362]]

Hyper parameter tuning of Gradient Boosting model

In [100]:
# param = {'gb__n_estimators': [150, 200],'gb__learning_rate': [0.01, 0.1, 0.2],'gb__max_depth': [3, 5, 7]}
# random_gb = RandomizedSearchCV(pipe_gb, param, cv=skf,n_jobs =-1)
# random_gb.fit(x_train, y_train)
# print("Best parameters for Gradient Boosting:", random_gb.best_params_)
In [101]:
# Best parameters for Gradient Boosting: {'gb__n_estimators': 200, 'gb__max_depth': 3, 'gb__learning_rate': 0.2}
# run time is too high for RandomizedSearchCV with above parameters so i have commented it
In [102]:
# Gradient Boosting Classifier After Hyperparameter Tuning
pipe_gb_tuned = Pipeline([
    ('scaler', StandardScaler()),
    ('gb', GradientBoostingClassifier(n_estimators=200, learning_rate=0.2, max_depth=3, random_state=42))
])
gb_cv_tuned = cross_val_score(pipe_gb_tuned, x_train, y_train, cv=skf).mean()
pipe_gb_tuned.fit(x_train, y_train)
gb_train_tuned = pipe_gb_tuned.score(x_train, y_train)
gb_test_tuned = pipe_gb_tuned.score(x_test, y_test)
gb_y_test_pred_tuned = pipe_gb_tuned.predict(x_test)
print('After Hyperparameter Tuning:')
print('Gradient Boosting Cross-Validation accuracy: ', gb_cv_tuned)
print('Gradient Boosting Training accuracy: ', gb_train_tuned)
print('Gradient Boosting Test accuracy: ', gb_test_tuned)
print('Gradient Boosting Classification Report: ', '\n', classification_report(y_test, gb_y_test_pred_tuned))
print('Gradient Boosting Confusion Matrix: ', '\n', confusion_matrix(y_test, gb_y_test_pred_tuned))

After Hyperparameter Tuning:
Gradient Boosting Cross-Validation accuracy:  0.5979513767795969
Gradient Boosting Training accuracy:  0.6069833237718382
Gradient Boosting Test accuracy:  0.5999490056093829
Gradient Boosting Classification Report:  
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.59      0.71      0.64     26071
           1       0.61      0.49      0.54     24915

    accuracy                           0.60     50986
   macro avg       0.60      0.60      0.59     50986
weighted avg       0.60      0.60      0.60     50986

Gradient Boosting Confusion Matrix:  
 [[18395  7676]
 [12721 12194]]

Comparing all methods and findings

1) Accuracy and Recall Value comparison

a) Logistic Regression Model: 59 percent with recall 0.49

b) Decision tree Model      : 60 percent with recall 0.46

c) Gradient Boost Model     : 60.7 percent with recall 0.49

2) From Confusion Matrices of all models, Logistic Regression predicted highest actual delays(12282) as compared to gradient boost (12194) and Decision Tree(10495).

2) Almost all models have accuracy around 60 %, This is expected because there are lot of other random factors which are not considered in the dataset.

a) weather issues like too much rain, water clogging on main runway  Visibility issues, too much Crosswind

b) Operational issues like Runway repairs, delay due to another airline which is still in boarding phase and hence lack of docking space,  or too much air traffic, busy taxiroutes, VIP(prime ministe/politician) planes preference for takeoff/landing

c) Emergency issues like: war,medical emergency landings,some technical/mechanical failures, goround policy by air traffice control

so ultimately these are all factors which can cause delays, So overall i think our model captures pattern very well and gives good accuracy.
